description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crustacean, likely a species of lobster or crayfish, featuring a segmented body with a robust cephalothorax and a long, muscular abdomen. The exoskeleton is predominantly brown with subtle variations in hue, and the surface appears hard and slightly rough. Prominent claws (chelae) are visible at the front, with one claw noticeably larger than the other, and multiple walking legs extend from the thoracic region. Two long, slender antennae project forward from the head, and the eyes are positioned on short stalks. The underside of the abdomen shows swimmerets, and the tail fans out at the rear. The overall form is elongated and laterally compressed, typical of aquatic crustaceans found in urban food markets or restaurants.
description: A brown, segmented crustacean with large claws, long antennae, and a hard exoskeleton. Its body is elongated with visible legs and a fan-shaped tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crustacean, both claws, and the extended antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the segmentation of the body, the symmetry of the legs, and the dorsal surface texture.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, with the claws and legs visible, and the tail fanned out.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the face, antennae, and the arrangement of the claws and legs.
